parsed_review.summary: CP00-417 is a clean descriptor-only increment of the RP13.P09 permission fixture slice (M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail of 9 rows + M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head of 1 row) over 10 planned units RP13.P09.M05.S12-RP13.P09.M06.S01. Coverage matches the live plan and queue exactly (deviation_from_plan:false; live_next_unit_id RP13.P09.M05.S12), distribution {implementation:8, claude_review:2} and micro-title {Permission And Audit Binding:9, Synthetic Fixture Set:1} are consistent across the contract, registry, service, validators, plan, and manifest. Every descriptor row carries descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false / customer_safe_errors_only:true / raw_payload_included:false, and the no-write attestation forbids payments/matching/AR-aging/journal runtime, audit/state/DB/object-storage writes, Hermes runtime receipts, and command runtime. The authority boundary is preserved (claude_is_final_approval:false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, human approval required to open runtime); Hermes H13 is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet, not product runtime. The pack does not over-claim production readiness (production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false; the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label). Tests and the rp13 validator exercise all seven CP417 exports and enforce contract-source deepEqual consistency, and command-evidence records npm test, rp13:payments-core:validate, and the full validation suite at exit 0 with CP392-CP416 coverage preserved. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON, non-zero-byte, well-formed), the forbidden_review_evidence list is complete, and there are no prior invalid review attempts. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One non-blocking P3 note: the post-review hardening artifacts (adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json) and post-review gated validations (closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) are not yet present/executed, which is by design since they are produced after this read-only review; the operator must ensure they complete and pass before commit, and this read-only PASS is not final human approval.

findings.title: Post-review hardening artifacts and gated validations pending (by design)
findings.details: The manifest lists five required pack artifacts (manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json), but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist on disk. The remaining three (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are generated by the deterministic post-review hardening sequence (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection) that runs after this read-only Claude review, so their absence at this stage is expected and not an implementation defect. Likewise, command-evidence.json notes that the post-review gated commands (npm run closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack-plan:generate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) are executed and recorded only before commit; they cannot be independently confirmed from a read-only inspection at this point. The pack-level production_ready claim is production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false (descriptor verified only), which is fully supported by the implementation, tests, and local/Hermes validation already present; the support contributions from adjudication and construction-inspection are produced downstream of this review.
findings.recommendation: Have the deterministic closeout pipeline generate the remaining required artifacts (claude-review-result.json from this review output, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and confirm the post-review gated validations (npm run closeout-pack:validate and git diff --check --cached) pass before committing CP00-417. Do not treat this read-only PASS as final human approval or as an enterprise trust claim.
findings.post_review_disposition: By design (pipeline ordering); non-blocking. Operator confirms remaining hardening steps complete and gated validations pass before commit; human final approval still required for any enterprise trust claim.
